text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Willow Creek, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver lived atop the branch of an ancient oak, where he had a splendid view of the meandering creek and the fields dotted with daisies. He was known not just for his wisdom, but also for his love of storytelling. Every autumn, the town would gather under Oliver’s wings for his annual storytelling festival.

This year, Oliver had a special tale. He began, “Once upon a moonlit night, there was a tiny village nestled among glowing crystals. The villagers spoke only a dozen languages, and their main export was laughter.”

A young girl, Emma, listened intently, her eyes wide with wonder. She whispered to her grandmother, “Is it true that everything is funny?”

Her grandmother chuckled, “Everything? Honey, all I know is laughter.”

That night, as the stars twinkled down on Willow Creek, Oliver spun a story filled with pirates plotting melons,
